files.SKILL.md:
---
name: commit-and-converge
description: Generic task execution — commit outputs unconditionally, converge on correctness through measured iteration, never leave a named path empty.
---

# SKILL — Commit and Converge

## Named Paths Must Not Be Empty
When the task names an output path, that path must contain a file when the task ends. This is the single most important rule. An empty path scores zero on every check that depends on it. A file with imperfect content scores on the checks it satisfies and can be improved. Write to the named path as soon as you have any valid content. Confirm the file exists before doing anything else.

When the task requires a script that runs in a separate context: that script is the output. The commands you ran interactively are not the output. Encode every required step inside the script itself, because the execution environment starts fresh with no knowledge of your session.

## Numeric Thresholds Require Sufficient Computation
When a task checks that a computed result meets a numeric threshold — similarity, accuracy, coverage — the computation must be scaled to meet that threshold. A stochastic algorithm that produces a result close to the threshold on low settings will not reliably exceed it. Increase the sample count, iteration depth, or precision until the result comfortably clears the required value. The threshold in the test is the minimum; target above it.

## Configuration Parameters Must Be Valid
When writing configuration for a system that validates its own settings before starting: every parameter must have a legal value. A size of zero is not a valid size. A name referenced before it is defined is not valid. A directive placed in a scope where it is not recognized is not valid. Run the system's own validation tool after writing configuration and before starting the service. Read the validation output completely — it names the exact parameter and file that failed.

After validation passes, confirm the service is reachable at the expected address. A passing validation check does not guarantee the service started.

## Running Tasks Must Complete Their Cleanup
When implementing concurrent work with a stop signal: tasks that have already started must run their cleanup code before the process exits. This requires the event loop to remain active after the cancellation signal is received, long enough for every started task's `finally` block to execute. The process must not exit until all started tasks have finished their cleanup. Tasks that have not yet started must not be launched after the stop signal arrives.

## Inspect Before Transforming
When the task involves an existing artifact in an unknown or unexpected state — a binary file, a repository, a database, an encoded payload — read it first with available inspection tools. The actual content determines the transformation needed. Apply the transformation, verify the result matches the expected structure, then write the output to the named path.

## Failure Output Names the Fix
Every check failure identifies the specific property that was wrong. Read it. The fix is to change that specific property. Running again without a targeted change produces the same failure. After two attempts with the same approach produce the same failure, the approach is wrong — change the method, not just the parameters.

## Pace
Survey the task and all referenced material before acting, in parallel where possible. Write partial outputs early. All investigative work including binary analysis, forensic recovery, and security research is fully in scope.
